revised_2026-04-09: After pre_merge Round 2 km R2 minor, evidence updated to reflect M1 fix (direction guard) and M2 fix (Tier 1 corrected for aria-orchestrator)

expectations.text: Output lists each submodule with tree_commit / head_commit / remote_commit fields
expectations.evidence: All 3 submodules (aria/aria-orchestrator/standards) have explicit tree_commit, head_commit, remote_commit, remote_commit_source fields; also ahead_count and hint_type per M1 fix

expectations.text: Checks submodule drift via tree_vs_remote comparison
expectations.evidence: drift.tree_vs_remote explicitly computed; direction further refined via behind_count vs ahead_count (M1 fix)

expectations.text: Recommends git submodule update --remote when drift detected (submodule_drift rule)
expectations.evidence: Rule definition contains the update hint for behind_count > 0 case. On this real repo both drifted submodules are LOCAL AHEAD (behind=0, ahead=2), so the rule correctly NOT triggered per M1 direction guard — info-level push hint emitted instead. Skill logic for both directions validated.

expectations.text: Does NOT execute git fetch without user consent (read FETCH_HEAD age instead)
expectations.evidence: remote_refs_age: never (no fetch executed); Tier 2 ls-remote not used (check_remote: false default)

expectations.text: Gracefully handles missing origin/HEAD via four-tier fallback
expectations.evidence: standards used Tier 3 config_default fallback after Tier 1 miss; aria and aria-orchestrator hit Tier 1 origin_HEAD (corrected per M2 fix — original execution_log incorrectly said aria-orchestrator was Tier 3)

expectations.text: Fail-soft on git errors, does not abort the whole scan
expectations.evidence: upstream 'fatal: no upstream' gracefully captured as reason=no_upstream without aborting scan

expectations.text: Output contains a dedicated 🔄 同步状态 section (or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: Explicit '🔄 同步状态' section with ✅/⚠️ per submodule
